question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction via ConstructionSubmit() with a crafted validStartNanos far in the past so that the mirror node records the transaction under a stale timestamp, causing the mirror node's chronological record to be incorrect or misaligned with actual consensus time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged user submit a signed transaction where TransactionGetTransactionHash() returns a hash that differs from what the consensus network actually records, causing the mirror node to index the transaction under a wrong hash and making it unfindable by legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged user craft a signed transaction that is accepted by TransactionExecute() but whose record is never propagated to the mirror node because the chosen node account ID (from getRandomNodeAccountId()) is a node that does not export records, resulting in a missing mirror node record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transactionSetTransactionId()] Can an unprivileged user supply validStartNanos=0 in metadata so that transactionSetTransactionId() calls TransactionIDGenerate() with a server-side timestamp, causing the mirror node to record a transaction ID that was never communicated back to the user, making the record effectively missing from the user's perspective?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged user supply a metadataKeyValidUntilNanos value that is only slightly in the future so that validStartNanos = validUntil - maxValidDurationNanos results in a transaction that expires before the mirror node ingests it, causing a missing record on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged user supply an extremely large metadataKeyValidUntilNanos string value that overflows int64 parsing in getIntMetadataValue(), causing validStartNanos to be computed incorrectly and the mirror node to record the transaction with a wrong timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged user omit metadataKeyValidUntilNanos and supply metadataKeyValidDurationSeconds=0 so that transactionSetValidDuration() defaults to 180 seconds, but also supply a metadataKeyValidStartNanos that is already expired, causing the transaction to be submitted but never confirmed and thus missing from mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transactionSetMemo()] Can an unprivileged user inject a crafted memo string via transactionSetMemo() that causes the mirror node to store an incorrect or truncated memo field, leading to incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transactionSetMemo()] Can an unprivileged user supply a memo value containing null bytes or special Unicode characters that are accepted by TransactionSetTransactionMemo() but cause the mirror node's record parser to truncate or corrupt the memo field in the exported record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction hex to ConstructionParse() where TransactionGetTransactionMemo() returns a memo that differs from what was originally set, causing the parsed record to reflect incorrect metadata compared to what the mirror node will export?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged user supply duplicate signatures in request.Signatures to ConstructionCombine() so that TransactionAddSignature() is called multiple times for the same key, causing the mirror node to record an incorrect signature set for the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged user supply a valid signature over frozenBodyBytes but for a public key that does not correspond to any authorized signer, causing the mirror node to record a transaction with an unexpected signer that was not part of the original operation, resulting in an incorrect record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrozenTransactionBodyBytes()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction whose prototext serialization via TransactionString() produces body bytes that differ from the actual transaction body used at consensus, causing the mirror node to record a transaction body that does not match what was signed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrozenTransactionBodyBytes()] Can an unprivileged user supply a transaction where prototext.Unmarshal() of the SignedTransaction succeeds but BodyBytes contains fields that are silently ignored by the mirror node's record parser, causing missing fields in the exported record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: unmarshallTransactionFromHexString()] Can an unprivileged user supply a hex-encoded transaction that decodes successfully via TransactionFromBytes() but contains extra or conflicting fields that cause the mirror node to export an incorrect record with wrong operation details?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: unmarshallTransactionFromHexString()] Can an unprivileged user supply a hex string with a valid 0x prefix that SafeRemoveHexPrefix() strips, but whose decoded bytes represent a transaction with a different type than intended, causing isSupportedTransactionType() to pass while the mirror node records an unexpected transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isSupportedTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that passes isSupportedTransactionType() as a TransferTransaction but whose internal fields encode a token transfer that the mirror node records differently than a plain HBAR transfer, causing incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSdkPayerAccountId()] Can an unprivileged user supply a crafted accountMapMetadata string where strings.Split(aliasMap, ':') produces more than two parts, causing mapping[1] to reference an unexpected segment and the mirror node to record the transaction under an incorrect payer account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSdkPayerAccountId()] Can an unprivileged user supply an accountMap string where the alias prefix match via strings.HasPrefix() is ambiguous (e.g., alias '0.0.1' matching '0.0.10'), causing the wrong account ID to be used as payer and the mirror node to record an incorrect payer in the transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSdkPayerAccountId()] Can an unprivileged user supply a comma-separated accountMap with a crafted entry where mapping[1] is an empty string, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString('') to succeed with a zero account, and the mirror node to record a transaction with a missing or zero payer account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveAccountAliases()] Can an unprivileged user supply a comma-separated list of account aliases in optionKeyAccountAliases where one alias resolves to a different account ID than expected due to a race condition in accountRepo.GetAccountId(), causing the mirror node to record a transaction with an incorrect account mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveAccountAliases()] Can an unprivileged user supply a very long comma-separated alias list in optionKeyAccountAliases to resolveAccountAliases(), causing repeated accountRepo.GetAccountId() calls that exhaust resources and result in partial alias resolution, leading to missing account mappings in the mirror node record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: resolveAccountAliases()] Can an unprivileged user supply duplicate aliases in the optionKeyAccountAliases string so that resolveAccountAliases() appends duplicate entries to accountMap, causing the mirror node to receive an ambiguous account map and record incorrect account associations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionMetadata()] Can an unprivileged user call ConstructionMetadata() with a crafted options map where optionKeyOperationType is a valid string but maps to a transaction type with an incorrect default max fee, causing the mirror node to record a transaction that fails due to insufficient fee and is thus missing from successful records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionMetadata()] Can an unprivileged user call ConstructionMetadata() repeatedly to obtain multiple valid metadataKeyValidUntilNanos values and then use an expired one in ConstructionPayloads(), causing the submitted transaction to be rejected at consensus and missing from mirror node records?
